questions.question: The hybridizations of atomic orbitals of nitrogen in NO_2^+, NO_3^- and NH_4^+ respectively are:
questions.question_raw: The hybridizations of atomic orbitals of nitrogen in NO2+, NO3- and NH4+ respectively are:
questions.explanation: The types of hybrid orbitals of nitrogen in NO_2^+, NO_3^–, and NH_4^+ respectively are expected to be sp, sp^2, and sp^3.

questions.question: The inertness of noble gas was observed to be due to their electronic configurations. Choose the most appropriate:
questions.question_raw: The inertness of noble gas was observed to be due to their electronic configurations. Choose the most appropriate:

options.label: A
options.text: Atomic mass is low so they are stable.

options.label: B
options.text: Total number of electrons in the outermost shell is duplets.

options.label: C
options.text: Outermost orbitals of the noble gases are fully filled.

options.label: D
options.text: Noble gases form ions to complete their outermost orbitals.

correct_option_texts: Outermost orbitals of the noble gases are fully filled.
questions.explanation: The six noble gases (Helium, Neon, Argon, Krypton, Xenon, Radon) are found in group 18 of the periodic table. These elements were considered to be inert gases until the 1960s because their oxidation number of 0 prevents the noble gases from forming compounds readily. All noble gases have the maximum number of electrons possible in their outer shell (2 for Helium, 8 for all others), making them stable.

questions.question: Stable outer octet of electrons is achieved in chlorine atom during the formation of NaCl by:
questions.question_raw: Stable outer octet of electrons is achieved in chlorine atom during the formation of NaCl by:

options.label: A
options.text: The formation of a hybrid orbital

options.label: C
options.text: The formation of a coordinate bond

questions.explanation: Na loses its one electron forming Na^+ while Cl gains the electron forming Cl^-.

questions.question: The bond formed, as a result of the electrostatic attraction between the positive and negative ions was termed as:
questions.question_raw: The bond formed, as a result of the electrostatic attraction between the positive and negative ions was termed as:
questions.explanation: The chemical bond formed between two atoms due to the transfer of electron(s) from one atom to the other. atom is called an "ionic bond" or "electrovalent bond". In an electrovalent bond one atom loses an electron to form a positive ion and the other atom gains the electron to form a negative ion. The resulting ions are held together by electrostatic attraction.

questions.question: The number of dots around the Lewis symbols for the elements represent:
questions.question_raw: The number of dots around the Lewis symbols for the elements represent:

options.label: B
options.text: the number of valence electrons of the element

correct_option_texts: the number of valence electrons of the element
questions.explanation:
Gilbert N. Lewis is widely known for his use of simple symbolic representations of elements that show valence electrons as dots.
The Lewis electron-dot symbols focus on the electrons in the highest principal energy level in the atom, the valence electrons.
After all, these are the electrons that participate in chemical reactions. Lewis electron-dot symbols work well for the representative elements.

questions.question: The formation of the Cl_2 molecule can be understood in terms of the sharing of a pair of electrons between the two chlorine atoms, each chlorine atom contributing one electron to the shared pair. Choose the most appropriate name of the bond that is formed:
questions.question_raw: The formation of the Cl2 molecule can be understood in terms of the sharing of a pair of electrons between the two chlorine atoms, each chlorine atom contributing one electron to the shared pair. Choose the most appropriate name of the bond that is formed:
questions.explanation: Two chlorine atoms will each share one electron to get a full outer shell and form a stable Cl_2 molecule. By sharing the two electrons where the shells touch each chlorine atom can count 8 electrons in its outer shell. These full outer shells with their shared electrons are now stable and the Cl_2 molecule will not react further with other chlorine atoms. One pair of shared electrons form a single covalent bond.

questions.question:
The electronic configurations of three elements, A, B and C are given below.
A 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6
B 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^2 3p^3
C 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6 3s^2 3p^5 
The molecular formula of the compound formed from B and C will be
questions.question_raw:
The electronic configurations of three elements, A, B and C are given below.
A 1s2 2s2 2p6
B 1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p3
C 1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p5 
The molecular formula of the compound formed from B and C will be
questions.explanation: B represents phosphorus (P) and C represents Chlorine (Cl). Thecompound formed is PCl_3 i.e., BC_3.

questions.question: 788 kJ of energy is required to separate one mole of solid NaCl into one mole of Na^+ (g) and one mole of Cl^- (g) to an infinite distance. This process explains:
questions.question_raw: 788 kJ of energy is required to separate one mole of solid NaCl into one mole of Na+ (g) and one mole of Cl- (g) to an infinite distance. This process explains:
questions.explanation: Lattice enthalpy is simply the change in Enthalpy associated with the formation of one mole of an ionic compound from its oppositely charged ions in their standard states under standard conditions.

questions.question: A qualitative measure of the stability of an ionic compound is provided by:
questions.question_raw: A qualitative measure of the stability of an ionic compound is provided by:
questions.explanation: Lattice energy is an estimate of the bond strength in ionic compounds. It is defined as the heat of formation for ions of opposite charge in the gas phase to combine into an ionic solid. The stability of the ionic bond is directly proportional to lattice energy.

questions.question: Bond lengths are lower in elements having:
questions.question_raw: Bond lengths are lower in elements having:
questions.explanation:
The bond length depends on the strength of the bond. The stronger the bond is, the shorter it will be. The triple bonds are the strongest and hence the shortest. Then comes double bonds which are of intermediate strength between the triple and single bonds. And finally, the single bonds are weaker than the other two. This way, Triple bonds are the shortest. Then comes double bonds. Finally, single bonds are the longest among the three.
The order of bond lengths is given as Triple bond < Double bond < Single bond.

questions.question: Bond angle helps us in:
questions.question_raw: Bond angle helps us in:

options.label: B
options.text: determining the reactivity of the element

options.label: C
options.text: determining the stability of the element

questions.explanation: Bond angles also contribute to the shape of a molecule. Bond angles are the angles between adjacent lines representing bonds. The bond angle can help differentiate between linear, trigonal planar, tetrahedral, trigonal-bipyramidal, and octahedral. The ideal bond angles are the angles that demonstrate the maximum angle where it would minimize repulsion, thus verifying the VSEPR theory.

questions.question: The amount of energy required to break one mole of bonds of a particular type between two atoms in a gaseous state is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The amount of energy required to break one mole of bonds of a particular type between two atoms in a gaseous state is called ________.
questions.explanation: Bond enthalpy also known as bond energy is the energy that is needed to break a particular bond in a gaseous compound. The unit that expresses bond enthalpy is kilojoules per mole or kJ/mol.

questions.question: H.O.H bond angle in water is:
questions.question_raw: H.O.H bond angle in water is:
questions.explanation: Due to the presence of two lone pairs on O in the H_2O bond angle reduce to 104.5^0 from 109^0.

questions.question: For N_2, bond order is:
questions.question_raw: For N2, bond order is:
questions.explanation: Bond order is the number of chemical bonds between a pair of atoms. in diatomic nitrogen N≡N, Triple covalent bond is present in nitrogen so the bond order is 3.

questions.question: N_2, CO, and NO^+ are isoelectronic molecules. Their respective bond order is:
questions.question_raw: N2, CO, and NO+ are isoelectronic molecules. Their respective bond order is:
questions.explanation:
Total number of electrons in N_2 molecule is 7+ 7= 14.
As per the formula Bonded pair of electrons N_b: $σ1s^2\ σ2s^2\ {tex}\pi$2p_y^2\ $\pi$2p_z^2\ σ 2p_x^2{/tex}
Total 10 electrons.
Anti bond pairs of electrons N_a: $σ 1s*^2\ σ 2s*^2$ Total 4 electrons.
Bond Order (B.O.) = ${N_b-N_a\over 2} ={ 10-4\over 2} = 3 $
Similarly bond order of CO And NO^+ is 3

questions.question: Structures of ${\text{CO}}_3^{2 - }$. ion given below explains the phenomenon of _____________.
questions.question_raw: Structures of {tex}{\text{CO}}_3^{2 - }{/tex}. ion given below explains the phenomenon of _____________.
questions.explanation: Resonance is the phenomenon when a molecule can be represented by 1 or more structure differing in the position of bonds and charge.

questions.question: The product of the magnitude of the charge and the distance between the centres of positive and negative charge is called _______.
questions.question_raw: The product of the magnitude of the charge and the distance between the centres of positive and negative charge is called _______.
questions.explanation: A dipole moment ​is a measurement of the separation of two opposite electrical charges. Dipole moments are a vector quantity. The magnitude is equal to the charge multiplied by the distance between the charges and the direction is from negative charge to positive charge: $\mu$ = q $\times$ r

questions.question: The valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory helps in the:
questions.question_raw: The valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory helps in the:

options.label: B
options.text: prediction of lewis structure of molecules

options.label: D
options.text: prediction of geometrical shapes of molecules

correct_option_texts: prediction of geometrical shapes of molecules
questions.explanation: Valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory is a model used in chemistry to predict the geometry of individual molecules from the number of electron pairs surrounding their central atoms. It is also named the Gillespie-Nyholm theory after its two main developers.

questions.question: In the formation of hydrogen molecule, the overlapping of atomic orbitals occur which results in the pairing of electrons. These are:
questions.question_raw: In the formation of hydrogen molecule, the overlapping of atomic orbitals occur which results in the pairing of electrons. These are:

options.label: A
options.text: valence shell electrons with same spins

options.label: B
options.text: valence shell electrons with opposite spins.

options.label: D
options.text: valence shell electrons irrespective of the spins

correct_option_texts: valence shell electrons with opposite spins.
questions.explanation: Overlapping of atomic orbitals having electrons of opposite spin take place in the formation of a molecule to cancel the dipole moment.

questions.question: In NO^-_3 ion, the number of bond pairs and lone pairs of electrons on nitrogen atom are
questions.question_raw: In NO-3 ion, the number of bond pairs and lone pairs of electrons on nitrogen atom are
questions.explanation:
The nitrate ion is formed by the loss of the hydrogen ion, and so its structure is:
Around the central nitrogen there are 4 pairs of shared electrons, and no remaining lone pair. The original lone pair has now become a bonding pair. Two of those pairs make up a double bond. The double bond unit and the two single bonds arrange themselves as far apart as possible in a trigonal planar arrangement - exactly the same as the carbonate ion.

questions.question: In case of a heteronuclear molecule like HF, the shared electron pair between the two atoms gets displaced more towards fluorine since the electronegativity of fluorine is far greater than that of hydrogen. This results in the molecule a
questions.question_raw: In case of a heteronuclear molecule like HF, the shared electron pair between the two atoms gets displaced more towards fluorine since the electronegativity of fluorine is far greater than that of hydrogen. This results in the molecule a
questions.explanation: because of slight shifting of electron towards more electronegative element like F in HF brings a small amount of negative charge on F and positive charge on H which results in dipole moment.

questions.question: Elements in which apart from 3s and 3p orbitals, 3d orbitals also available for bonding In a number of compounds of these elements there are more than eight valence electrons around the central atom. One such example is:
questions.question_raw: Elements in which apart from 3s and 3p orbitals, 3d orbitals also available for bonding In a number of compounds of these elements there are more than eight valence electrons around the central atom. One such example is:
questions.explanation: In H_2SO_4, S has more than 8 electrons in the valence shell. It has 12 electrons in its valence shell.

questions.question: In BF_3, molecule below the dipole moment is zero although the B - F bonds are oriented at an angle of 120° to one another. Net dipole moment in BF_3, molecule is
questions.question_raw: In BF3, molecule below the dipole moment is zero although the B - F bonds are oriented at an angle of 120° to one another. Net dipole moment in BF3, molecule is
questions.explanation: BF_3 has symmetrical structure in which the three B-F bonds are oriented at an angle of 120° to one another. Also the three bonds lie in one plane and the dipole moments of these bonds cancel one another giving net dipole moment equal to zero.

questions.question: Ionic crystalline compounds formed by ion-formation by electron transfer proves:
questions.question_raw: Ionic crystalline compounds formed by ion-formation by electron transfer proves:

options.label: C
options.text: Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion VSEPR Theory

questions.explanation: Ionic crystalline compounds formed by ion-formation by electron transfer proves the electronic theory of chemical bonding. According to this theory, ionic bonds are formed when an atom loses electron/ electrons to become a positive ion and another atom gains the electron/ electrons to become a negative ion. Ionic bonding is a process of complete transfer of Valence electrons.

questions.question: The tetrahedral molecule below where there are two lone pairs of electrons is
questions.question_raw: The tetrahedral molecule below where there are two lone pairs of electrons is
questions.explanation: An important feature of water is its polar nature. The structure has a bent molecular geometry for the two hydrogens from the oxygen vertex. The oxygen atom also has two lone pairs of electrons.

questions.question: The criterion of overlap of atomic orbitals is the main factor for the formation of:
questions.question_raw: The criterion of overlap of atomic orbitals is the main factor for the formation of:
questions.explanation:
The valence bond theory is introduced to describe the bonding in organic molecules. In this model, bonds are considered to form from the overlapping of two atomic orbitals on different atoms, each orbital containing a single electron.
In general, greater the overlap, stronger is the bond formed between the two atoms. Thus, according to the orbital overlap concept, atoms combine together by overlapping their orbital and thus forming a lower energy state where their valence electrons with opposite spin, pair up to form the covalent bond.

questions.question: The octet of electrons represents a particularly stable electronic arrangement. Atoms achieve a stable octet when they are linked by chemical bonds. This rule is associated with which one of the following theories?
questions.question_raw: The octet of electrons represents a particularly stable electronic arrangement. Atoms achieve a stable octet when they are linked by chemical bonds. This rule is associated with which one of the following theories?

options.label: B
options.text: Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion VSEPR Theory

questions.explanation:
G.N.Lewis, proposed the octet rule to explain the valence electron sharing between atoms that resulted in a bonding type with the atoms attaining noble gas electronic configuration.
The statement is: "a bond is formed between two atoms by mutual sharing of pairs of electrons to attain a stable outer-octet of electrons for each atom involved in bonding".

tests.testPaperName: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure CBSE Online Test 03

questions.question: The maximum number of hydrogen bonds that a molecule of water can have is
questions.question_raw: The maximum number of hydrogen bonds that a molecule of water can have is
questions.explanation: The two hydrogens of the water molecule can form hydrogen bonds with other oxygens in water, and the two lone pair of electrons on oxygen of the water molecule can attract other hydrogens in water. Hence, 4 possible hydrogen bonds.

questions.question: During the formation of a chemical bond:
questions.question_raw: During the formation of a chemical bond:

options.label: A
options.text: energy of the system does not change

options.label: B
options.text: electron-electron repulsion becomes more than the nucleus-electron attraction

questions.explanation: A chemical bond is a lasting attraction between atoms that enables the formation of chemical compounds. The bond may result from the electrostatic force of attraction between atoms with opposite charges, or through the sharing of electrons as in the covalent bonds. When a bond forms, electrons are attracted to the space between nuclei where the electrostatic force of attraction is greater. As the electrons fall to a position of lower potential energy, the total mechanical energy of the molecular system decreases. Part of the mechanical energy of the unbound atoms is lost when they form the molecular system with a lower total mechanical energy.

questions.question: Which of the following has maximum number of lone pairs associated with Xe?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following has maximum number of lone pairs associated with Xe?
questions.explanation:
In XeF_​​​​2: Xe has 2 of its electrons in bonding with F. So, since Xe has 8 valence electrons, the no.of lone pairs = ${no. \ of \ non-bonding \ electrons\over 2}={6\over 2}=3$
 In XeO_3: Xe has 6 of its electrons in bonding with O (bcoz of double bond). So, Xe will have 2 non bonding electrons and therefore 1 lone pair.
 In XeF_4: Xe has 4 of its electrons in bonding with F. So, Xe will have 4 non bonding electrons and therefore 2 lone pairs.
 In XeF_6: Xe has 6 of its electrons in bonding with F. So, Xe will have 2 non bonding electrons and therefore 1 lone pair.
So XeF_2 has the maximum no. of lone pairs associated with Xe.

questions.question_raw: The structure of IF7 is

questions.question_raw: The number of types of bonds between two carbon atoms in calcium carbide is
questions.explanation: CaC_2 has a combination of bonds. It is an ionic lattice that has Ca^2+ cations and acetylide C_2^2- anions. Within each C_2^2-there is a triple bond between the 2C atoms, consisting of 1 sigma and 2 pi bonds.

questions.question: The hybridization of orbitals of N atom in ${\text{NO}}_3^ - $, ${\text{NO}}_2^ + $ and $NH_4^ + $ are respectively:
questions.question_raw: The hybridization of orbitals of N atom in {tex}{\text{NO}}_3^ - {/tex}, {tex}{\text{NO}}_2^ + {/tex} and {tex}NH_4^ + {/tex} are respectively:
questions.explanation: Hybridization of orbitals of N atom in ${\text{NO}}_3^ - $, ${\text{NO}}_2^ + $ and ${\text{N}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{4}}}^ + $are sp^2, sp, sp^3 which can be explained by their Lewis structures. The empty p- orbitals of N take part in hybridization.

questions.question: Among the following, the maximum covalent character is shown by the compound is:
questions.question_raw: Among the following, the maximum covalent character is shown by the compound is:
questions.explanation:
Few ionic bonds have partial covalent characteristics which were first discussed by a scientist, Kazimierz Fajans in 1923. He gave the rule to predict whether a chemical bond will be covalent or ionic which is now commonly known as Fajan’s rule. The rule can be stated as:
 Size of the ion: Smaller the size of cation, larger the size of anion, greater is the covalent character of the ionic bond.
 Charge of Cation: Greater the charge of cation, greater is the covalent character of the ionic bond.
 Electronic configuration- For cations with the same charge and size, the one, with (n-1)d^nns^​​​0 which is found in transition elements, has greater covalent character than the cation with ns^2np^6 electronic configuration, which is commonly found in alkali or alkaline earth metals.
Keeping these rules in mind, we can say that AlCl_​​​3 has a high covalent character.

questions.question: Which one of the following is paramagnetic?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is paramagnetic?
questions.explanation: The number of electrons in Nitrogen is 7 and in oxygen is 8. Hence the number of electrons that would be present in the molecular orbitals in NO is 7 + 8 = 15. As the number of electrons is odd, all the electrons in the NO molecule cannot be paired. Hence, a single electron would be present in a $\pi$*2p orbital. Therefore NO is an odd electron species and the gas is hence paramagnetic due to the presence of unpaired electron.

questions.question: Hybridization of C_2 and C_3 of H_3C - CH = C = Ch - CH_3 are:
questions.question_raw: Hybridization of C2 and C3 of H3C - CH = C = Ch - CH3 are:
questions.explanation: C_2 is sp^2 while C_3 is sp (a special case of allenes).

questions.question: When ${{\text{O}}_{\text{2}}}$ is converted into ${{\text{O}}_{\text{2}}}^ + $
questions.question_raw: When {tex}{{\text{O}}_{\text{2}}}{/tex} is converted into {tex}{{\text{O}}_{\text{2}}}^ + {/tex}

options.label: B
options.text: paramagnetic character decreases and the bond order increases

options.label: D
options.text: both paramagnetic character and bond order increase

correct_option_texts: paramagnetic character decreases and the bond order increases
questions.explanation:
For O_2: $\sigma$1s^2, $\sigma$*1s^2, $\sigma$2s^2, $\sigma$*2s^2, $\sigma$2pz^2 
($\pi$2py^2 = $\pi$2px^2), ($\pi$*2py^1 = $\pi$*2px^1)$\sigma$*2pz
For O_2: $\sigma$1s^2, $\sigma$*1s^2, $\sigma$2s^2, $\sigma$*2s^2, $\sigma$2pz^2 
($\pi$2py^2 = $\pi$2px^2), ($\pi$*2py^1 = $\pi$*2px^0)$\sigma$*2pz
Bond order = $N_b-N_a\over 2 $
For O_2 = $10-6\over 2$ = 2
For O_2^+ = $10-5\over 2$= 2.5

questions.question: Which one of the following conversions involve change in both hybridization and shape?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following conversions involve change in both hybridization and shape?
questions.explanation: BF_3 has sp^2 hybridization and its shape is a triangular planar. While BF_4^- has sp^3 hybridization and shape is tetrahedral.

questions.question: Based on VSEPR theory, the number of 90^0 F — Br — F angles in BrF_5 is:
questions.question_raw: Based on VSEPR theory, the number of 900 F — Br — F angles in BrF5 is:
questions.explanation: The steric number of BrF_5 is 6. So the geometry is octahedral. It will have 5 bps and one lp which is present in the axial position. Because of distortion caused by lp, there is no 90^o F—Br—F bond angle in BrF_5.

questions.question: The species having pyramidal shape is:

questions.question: For a stable molecule the value of bond order must be:
questions.question_raw: For a stable molecule the value of bond order must be:

options.label: C
options.text: there is no relationship between stability and bond order

questions.explanation:
Bond order $\propto $ Stability
Hence, for a stable molecule, the value of bond order must be positive. When bond order is zero and negative the molecule will not exist.

questions.question: Using MO theory predicts which of the following species has the shortest bond length?

questions.explanation: Bond length is inversely proportional to bond order. $O_2^{2 + }$ has highest bond order as electrons are removed from antibonding MO.

questions.question: Which one of the following pairs of species has the same bond order?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following pairs of species has the same bond order?
questions.explanation:
Bond order=${n_b−n_a\over 2}$
In CN^-, there are 8 bonding electrons and 2 antibonding electrons,
Therefore B.O.=$8−2\over 2$ = 3
NO^+ has 10 electrons, a triple bond between N and O, and two pairs, one on each atom. This means that both N and O follow the octet rule. The bond order is 3.

questions.question: Which of the following attain the linear structure?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following attain the linear structure?
questions.explanation:
Molecular geometry is determined by the hybridization of the central atom. In CS_2 the carbon atom is surrounded by two regions of electron density, one for each double bond it forms with the sulfur atoms. The molecular geometry will thus be linear, the basic AX_2 model.
The molecular geometry of BeCl_2 is linear with symmetric charge distribution around the central atom.

questions.question: Which of the following species have the same shape?

questions.question: Hydrogen bonds are formed in many compounds e.g., H_2O, HF, NH_3. The boiling point of such compounds depends to a large extent on the strength of the hydrogen bond and the number of hydrogen bonds. The correct decreasing order of the boiling points of the above compounds is:
questions.question_raw: Hydrogen bonds are formed in many compounds e.g., H2O, HF, NH3. The boiling point of such compounds depends to a large extent on the strength of the hydrogen bond and the number of hydrogen bonds. The correct decreasing order of the boiling points of the above compounds is:
questions.explanation:
The smaller the size of the atom, the greater is the electronegativity, and hence stronger is the H−bonding. Thus, the order of strength of H-bonding is HF > H_2O > NH_3. 
But each HF molecule is linked only to two other HF molecules while each H2​O molecule is linked to four other H2​O molecules through H−bonding.
Hence, the decreasing order of boiling points is H_2​O > HF > NH_3.

questions.question: The types of hybrid orbitals of nitrogen in ${\text{NO}}_2^ + $, ${\text{NO}}_3^ - $ and $N{\text{H}}_4^ + $ respectively are expected to be
questions.question_raw: The types of hybrid orbitals of nitrogen in {tex}{\text{NO}}_2^ + {/tex}, {tex}{\text{NO}}_3^ - {/tex} and {tex}N{\text{H}}_4^ + {/tex} respectively are expected to be
questions.explanation:
In NO_2^+
 Number of electron pairs =2
 Number of bond pairs = 2
 Number of lone pair = 0
 So, the species is linear with sp hybridisation.
 In $NO_3^− $
 Number of electron pairs =3
 Number of bond pairs = 3
 Number of lone pair = 0
 So, the species is trigonal planar with sp​​​​2 hybridisation
 In $NH_4^+$
 Number of electron pairs =4
 Number of bond pairs = 4
 Number of lone pair = 0
 So, the species is tetrahedral with sp^3 hybridisation.

questions.explanation: BF_4^- and NH_4^+ both have sp^3 hybridisation.

questions.question: In ${\text{NO}}_3^ - $ ion, the number of bond pairs and lone pairs of electrons on nitrogen atom are
questions.question_raw: In {tex}{\text{NO}}_3^ - {/tex} ion, the number of bond pairs and lone pairs of electrons on nitrogen atom are
questions.explanation:
The nitrate ion is formed by the loss of the hydrogen ion, and so its structure is:
Around the central nitrogen there are 4 pairs of shared electrons, and no remaining lone pair. The original lone pair has now become a bonding pair. Two of those pairs make up a double bond.

questions.question: Amongst the following elements whose electronic configurations are given below, the one having the highest ionisation enthalpy is
questions.question_raw: Amongst the following elements whose electronic configurations are given below, the one having the highest ionisation enthalpy is
questions.explanation: $[Ne]3s^23p^3$ and $[Ar]3d^{10}4s^24p^3$ both have stable half-filled configuration but in $[Ne]3s^23p^3$ the atomic radii are smaller than $[Ar]3d^{10}4s^24p^3$. So ionization enthalpy of $[Ne]3s^23p^3$ is highest amongst all.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are not correct?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following statements are not correct?

options.label: A
options.text: In canonical structures, there is no difference in the arrangement of atoms.

options.label: B
options.text: NaCl being an ionic compound is a good conductor of electricity in the solid state.

options.label: C
options.text: Hybrid orbitals form stronger bonds than pure orbitals.

options.label: D
options.text: VSEPR Theory can explain the square planar geometry of XeF_4.

correct_option_texts: NaCl being an ionic compound is a good conductor of electricity in the solid state.
questions.explanation: NaCl is not a good conductor in solid-state. NaCl is a good conductor of electricity only in their molten state or in aqueous solution.

questions.question: Rank the bonds in the set C=O, C-O, C $ \equiv $ O in order of decreasing bond length.
questions.question_raw: Rank the bonds in the set C=O, C-O, C {tex} \equiv {/tex} O in order of decreasing bond length.
questions.explanation: The length of the bond is determined by the number of bonded electrons (the bond order). The higher the bond order, the stronger the pull between the two atoms and the shorter the bond length. Generally, the length of the bond between two atoms is approximately the sum of the covalent radii of the two atoms. CO has a triple bond so has minimum bond length.

questions.question: Rank the bonds in the set C=O, C-O, C$ \equiv $O in order of decreasing bond strength.
questions.question_raw: Rank the bonds in the set C=O, C-O, C{tex} \equiv {/tex}O in order of decreasing bond strength.
questions.explanation: The more bonds between atoms, the shorter the bond is. For example, a triple bond will be shorter than a double bond. The shorter the bond is the stronger it is (higher dissociation energy). Bond order is basically the number of bonds between atoms, so a larger number bond order=more bonds=shorter bonds=stronger bonds. i.e., C$ \equiv $O > C=O > C-O.

questions.question: Quartz is very hard and melts at 1550°C. Reason is:
questions.question_raw: Quartz is very hard and melts at 1550°C. Reason is:

options.label: A
options.text: Quartz (SiO_2) has silicon-oxygen covalent bonds that extend throughout the sample

options.label: B
options.text: separate SiO_2 molecules exist that are held together by pi bonds that extend in three dimensions throughout the sample

options.label: C
options.text: Quartz (SiO_2) has silicon-oxygen electrovalent bonds that extend throughout the sample

options.label: D
options.text: separate SiO_2 molecules exist they are held together by coordinate bonds that extend throughout the sample

correct_option_texts: Quartz (SiO_2) has silicon-oxygen covalent bonds that extend throughout the sample
questions.explanation: It's because quartz has a network of covalent bonds which means that they are covalently bonded in a network solid. The many covalent bonds must be broken which is why they have very high melting points.

questions.question: The electronegativity difference $\left( {\Delta {\text{EN}}} \right)$ is large in one of the following:
questions.question_raw: The electronegativity difference {tex}\left( {\Delta {\text{EN}}} \right){/tex} is large in one of the following:
questions.explanation: Metals are electropositive in nature as they easily lose electrons, so they are reducing agents. On the contrary, Non-metals are electronegative because they gain electrons and thus they are oxidizing agents.

questions.question: N_2, CO and NO^+ are isoelectronic molecules. Their respective bond order is :
questions.question_raw: N2, CO and NO+ are isoelectronic molecules. Their respective bond order is :
questions.explanation:
Total number of electrons in the N_2 molecule is 7+ 7= 14.
As per the formula Bonded pair of electrons N_b: $σ1s^2\ σ2s^2\ {tex}\pi$2p_y^2\ $\pi$2p_z^2\ σ 2p_x^2{/tex}
Total 10 electrons.
Anti bond pairs of electrons N_a: $σ 1s*^2\ σ 2s*^2$ Total 4 electrons.
Bond Order (B.O.) = ${N_b-N_a\over 2} ={ 10-4\over 2} = 3 $
Similarly, the bond order of CO and NO^+ is 3.

questions.question: Bond order of ${\text{O}}_2^ + $ and ${\text{O}}_2^ - $ are:
questions.question_raw: Bond order of {tex}{\text{O}}_2^ + {/tex} and {tex}{\text{O}}_2^ - {/tex} are:
questions.explanation:
The electronic configuration of the O_2^+ ion containing 15 electrons can be written as: $\sigma $1s^2< $\sigma $*1s^2< $\sigma $2s^2< $\sigma $*2s^2< $\sigma $2p_z^2 < ($\pi $2p_x^2= $\pi $2p_y^2) < ($\pi $*2p_x^1= $\pi $*2p_y) < $\sigma $*2p_z
The bond order can be found as:
B.O = $N_b-N_a\over 2$
N_b= Number of electrons in the bonding orbitals = 10
N_a = Number of electron in the anti-bonding orbitals = 5
B.O = ^${10-5\over 2 }= {5\over 2 }$= 2.5
The electronic configuration of the O_2^- ion containing 17 electrons can be written as: $\sigma $1s^2< $\sigma $*1s^2< $\sigma $2s^2< $\sigma $*2s^2< $\sigma $2p_z^2 < ($\pi $2p_x^2 = $\pi $2p_y^2) < ($\pi $*2p_x^2= $\pi $*2p_y^1) < σ*2p_z
The bond order can be calculated as: B.O = ${10-7\over 2} ={ 3\over 2}$= 1.5

questions.question: NaCl does not conduct electricity in the ____________ but do conduct it when melted.
questions.question_raw: NaCl does not conduct electricity in the ____________ but do conduct it when melted.
questions.explanation: The essential requirement for conducting electricity is the presence of free electrons or mobile/free ions. Sodium chloride in the solid-state is closely packed by the strong electrostatic force of attraction and the ions are immobile. Therefore as there is no mobile ions conduction of electricity is not possible. But ionic compounds in molten state ions have enough energy to break the electrostatic forces of attraction and become mobile therefore conduct electricity.
